name: Huaynaputina
sources_notes: Largest South American eruption in recorded history; global cooling

name: Vesuvius
sources_notes: VEI 5 sub-Plinian; killed 4000+ in Naples region

name: Krakatau (Krakatoa)
sources_notes: Sound heard 4800km away; tsunami killed most victims

name: Mount Pelée (Martinique)
sources_notes: VEI 4 but high death toll; Saint-Pierre destroyed; technically below VEI 5 cutoff but included for impact

name: Bezymianny
sources_notes: Smithsonian GVP; instructive analog for Mt St Helens

name: Mount Ruapehu
sources_notes: Below VEI 5 but extended eruption; excluded from primary analysis

name: Chaitén
sources_notes: Sub-VEI 5 but rapid reawakening of long-dormant volcano

name: Eyjafjallajökull
sources_notes: Major aviation disruption Europe; VEI 4 (below cutoff but notable)

name: Hunga Tonga-Hunga Ha'apai
sources_notes: Tsunami; largest atmospheric blast since 1883 Krakatau; ~6 deaths
